items.title: 'Heart ripped out on repeat': Auskick parents say were failed by AFL
items.summary: Several families tell the ABC they have been failed by the AFL Integrity Unit following bullying, racism and harassment.

items.title: Warm winter outlook not bothering ski resorts as 2026 snow season looms
items.summary: Alpine business owners and resort managers say they are not overly concerned about a warmer winter forecast, with preparations underway for the 2026 ski season ahead of the King's Birthday long weekend.

items.title: Australians limited to keyhole view of Bondi attack scrutiny
items.summary: For now, Australians seeking to understand any intelligence gaps, failures, missed warnings and government failings before the Bondi massacre are expected to accept a keyhole view.

items.title: Best new books out in May from Elizabeth Strout, Francesca Albanese and more
items.summary: Sweet teen love, gay football stars, alien abductions and a murderous PhD student bent on revenge — this month's best new releases have it all.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of John Farnham, 60s pop and where to live on the Moon
items.summary: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: Canadian man Kenneth Law, accused of selling lethal chemical online, pleads guilty to aiding suicide
items.summary: In 2023, Interpol alerted Australian police that packages sold by the man, which contained a lethal substance, were sent to Australia, prompting authorities to check on the welfare of recipients.

items.title: WHO says 906 suspected cases and 223 suspected deaths from Bundibugyo strain of Ebola
items.summary: The outbreak is threatening to overwhelm health authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo, while nine cases have now been confirmed in neighbouring Uganda.

items.title: Romanian President Nicusor Dan calls defence council meeting over 'unprecedented' Russian drone crash
items.summary: A Russian drone has struck an apartment building in NATO-member Romania, wounding two people.

items.title: Blues rookie in doubt for Origin II after injury in Cronulla's win over Manly
items.summary: New South Wales are set to be without exciting hooker Blayke Brailey after he suffered a suspected broken arm in Cronulla's 28-22 win over Manly.

items.title: Kids still see gambling ads during late night sport under Labor
items.summary: The partial ad ban will not prevent children from seeing gambling ads during footy or rugby games, Labor's draft legislation shows.

items.title: Friday finance with Lin Lin
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Scott calls for high ARC standards, laments missed 'howler' in loss
items.summary: Geelong's coach takes aim at the AFL's controversial review technology after yet another blunder cost his side dearly in the loss to Carlton.

items.title: Former police officer's messages did not amount to 'implied admissions' of rape, court told
items.summary: A woman who says she was raped by a former South Australian police officer did not confront her alleged attacker "because she knew it wasn't true", a court has heard.

items.title: NT Aboriginal families in court bid to be recognised as traditional owners of Cobourg Peninsula
items.summary: Two groups of Indigenous families have claimed in the Federal Court they should be recognised as the traditional owners of the NT's Cobourg Peninsula.

items.title: New Tennis Australia CEO Andrew Abdo unlikely to be grand slam boss
items.summary: Incoming Tennis Australia CEO Andrew Abdo appears unlikely to take on the double workload of his predecessor Craig Tiley, by running both the national organisation and the Australian Open tournament.

items.title: Former prime minister Tony Abbott elected unopposed as Liberal Party president
items.summary: Former prime minister Tony Abbott says he is ready to serve the Liberal Party in its time of 'existential crisis' upon his return to the political stage.

items.title: Bumper year at Northern Territory's biggest bull sale delivers average price of nearly $5,000 a head
items.summary: The Northern Territory's biggest bull sale has recorded one of its strongest results, despite high fuel prices and a big wet season.

items.title: Award-winning volunteer Jamal Box bailed on child-abuse image charges
items.summary: A former police staffer and lauded SES volunteer is granted bail on a charge of possessing child-abuse images.

items.title: Mouse plague taskforce to help WA country schools battling infestation
items.summary: COVID-style cleaning measures have returned as WA's ongoing mouse plague spreads across multiple country schools.

items.title: AFL backs controversial decision to disallow Hawks' goal against Saints
items.summary: A controversial umpiring decision that disallowed Hawthorn star Nick Watson's goal against St Kilda has been backed by the AFL.

items.title: NSW coroner absolves police of blame for pursuit that led to death on Pacific Motorway
items.summary: NSW Deputy State Coroner David O'Neil finds police acted within reason during a pursuit that led to a man being killed on the Pacific Motorway, north of Byron Bay.

items.title: Plummeting gold price impacts Australian dollar as Iran war pushes it into bear market
items.summary: The gold price is now plunging after the precious metal's surge to a record high this year.

items.title: FIFA World Cup selection for Michael Woud forces wedding postponement
items.summary: Auckland FC goalkeeper Michael Woud has had to put his wedding plans on hold after being selected in New Zealand's squad for the World Cup, but he says taking part in soccer's biggest tournament is not a bad reason to cancel.

items.title: Friends, loyal listeners gather for James Valentine's public memorial
items.summary: The veteran broadcaster's family, friends and loyal listeners gathered in Sydney Town Hall to pay tribute to the 64-year-old who died last month, two years after he was diagnosed with cancer.

items.title: Drag queen Pattie Gonia says Patagonia has launched trademark suit. Here's what we know
items.summary: A drag queen and a major outdoor clothing brand have a fight over trademarks. Here's the feud explained.

items.title: Victorian state MP Jacinta Ermacora apologises for mistakenly saying alleged koala killer had died
items.summary: State MP Jacinta Ermacora says "they were misinformed and passed that misinformation on".

items.title: Parramatta Eels reveal Mitchell Moses injury outlook ahead of State of Origin II
items.summary: Parramatta do not have any concerns with the handling of Mitchell Moses's fitness in the NSW Blues set-up, as the Eels skipper faces a race against the clock to play before Game II of State of Origin.

items.title: Victorian coroner finds earlier hospital transfer could have prevented baby's death after home birth
items.summary: A Victorian coroner has found a baby's death could have been avoided if his mother had been transferred to hospital hours earlier, after signs of an obstructed labour.

items.title: Taylors Wines considers moving SA business overseas if tax changes go through
items.summary: The managing director of Taylors Wines in South Australia is considering leaving the country in response to the federal government's proposed tax changes.

items.title: Family in 'forever home' pleads for changes to Jervis Bay Road upgrade
items.summary: After years spent modifying their Shoalhaven property to accommodate their son's special care needs, the Taylors fear noise, lights and disruption from a major road project could affect his health.

items.title: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket explodes during test fire

items.title: KPMG boss resigns over mishandled whistleblower allegations
items.summary: KPMG Australia chief executive Andrew Yates has stepped down amid a scandal involving audit partners accessing confidential client documents.

items.title: Rising costs threaten eisteddfods across Australia
items.summary: The Centralian Eisteddfod Council has raised the alarm about funding challenges, which could mean the event will cease after next year.

items.title: Attorney-general defends blocking counterterror documents from Bondi royal commission
items.summary: The federal government has made a public interest immunity claim over the documents before the Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, essentially arguing it is not in the public interest for the documents to be released.

items.title: Residents in Lebanon's south caught in the middle as fighting again intensifies
items.summary: New waves of missile strikes and vast advisories from the Israeli government to evacuate have left residents in Lebanon's south uncertain of where to turn.

items.title: John Farnham tribute concert to be held in September at Melbourne's Rod Laver Arena
items.summary: Jimmy Barnes, Tina Arena and Human Nature will take the stage to honour Australian legend John Farnham at a Melbourne tribute concert later this year.

items.title: Five arrests as police and protesters clash at Victoria Park amid Olympic stadium dispute
items.summary: Five people were taken into custody and a protest camp was broken up at Victoria Park ahead of construction beginning on the $3.6 billion Olympic stadium development.

items.title: Afghan Refugee Women's XI Cricket Tour of Darwin spotlights need for gender equality in sport
items.summary: After the Taliban banned women's sport in Afghanistan, most of the women's cricket team ended up relocating to Australia. Now, they're on tour in the Northern Territory — "a happy ending" after a traumatic journey.

items.title: Jannik Sinner's French Open loss to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo leaves rivals stunned
items.summary: It seemed a foregone conclusion Jannik Sinner would win the French Open, which is why his meltdown amid the Paris heat wave was so unexpected.

items.title: New drivers inducted in Rockhampton for the last working Purrey steam tram in the world
items.summary: Steam-powered trams ran in Rockhampton for 30 years until 1939 and are now displayed at a volunteer museum, between their Sunday and school holiday trips.

items.title: Claims funding promise to cancer patients missing from Tasmanian state budget
items.summary: A cancer survivor who stood alongside the Tasmanian premier at a 2025 election promise announcement says he is disappointed that the hospitals investment appears to be "on the back burner."

items.title: Comedian Will Gibb on the perils of TikTok fame
items.summary: As a comedian with legions of fans on social media, Will Gibb wishes he didn't still feel the need to legitimise himself. But he does.

items.title: Diphtheria vaccine push in WA's north as authorities battle 'disease of the past' disinformation
items.summary: Health authorities in Western Australia's far north say they are battling disinformation and misconceptions amid a vaccination drive to contain a diphtheria outbreak.

items.title: ACCC sues Amazon over alleged non-compliance with button battery warnings in kids backpacks
items.summary: The consumer watchdog is suing Amazon alleging it sold children's unicorn backpacks on its online marketplace that failed to comply with mandatory button battery safety warnings.

items.title: NSW skipper Isabelle Kelly 'speechless' after Blues make Women's State of Origin history
items.summary: NSW captain Isabelle Kelly praises her Blues teammates after they complete a historic 3-0 State of Origin whitewash against Queensland.

items.title: Yindjibarndi leader not ready to accept $150m native title payout from Andrew Forrest's Fortescue
items.summary: More than a fortnight after winning the largest native title compensation payout ever in Australia, Pilbara leader Michael Woodley returns to his country.

items.title: From Kneecap to Ecca Vandal and Genesis Owusu — these are May's best albums
items.summary: From long-awaited comebacks to First Nations glam rock and genre-defying polymaths, May was stacked with excellent new releases.

items.title: Auction clearance rates at 2022 housing downturn levels, ASX up — as it happened
items.summary: Auction clearance rates have fallen to levels not seen since the 2022 housing downturn. The ASX closed up 1.6 per cent on hopes of a Middle East peace deal.

items.title: Australian Border Force seize 20 million illegal vapes since January 2024 as crackdown continues
items.summary: More than 20 million illegal vapes, with a street value of just over $1 billion, have been seized by law enforcement authorities since Australia's vaping reforms came into effect.

items.title: Australia's Daria Kasatkina sets up French Open clash with Aryna Sabalenka in third round
items.summary: Daria Kasatkina will face world number one Aryna Sabalenka in the third round as fellow Australian Kimberly Birrell departs the singles draw in Paris.

items.title: Darwin Harbour Middle Arm gas and petrochemical precinct could be roadblocked by endangered herb
items.summary: The NT government is arguing its planned mega Middle Arm gas industry precinct will be needed to help shore up Australia's future fuel and energy supplies — but it has just identified that a tiny endangered herb could be growing in the way.

items.title: Kids protest against fast-food giant after McDonald's buys Castlemaine land for $4.6m
items.summary: Children turn out in protest after it's revealed the global fast-food giant has paid $4.6 million for a prominent site in central Victoria's Castlemaine.

items.title: Canberra man jailed over sexual abuse of 'extremely vulnerable' child in 2024
items.summary: A man who sexually assaulted a 12-year-old girl after a "chance encounter" at a Canberra shopping centre has been jailed, with the judge finding he took advantage of an "extremely vulnerable" child.

items.title: Jannik Sinner exits French Open in five-set loss to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo in Paris
items.summary: Jannik Sinner's French Open campaign is over after the world number one struggles with illness in a shock second-round defeat to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo.

items.title: Benjamin Netanyahu directs Israel's military to take over 70 per cent of Gaza
items.summary: Israel effectively controls an estimated 64 per cent of Gaza, now bombarded to ruins by Israel's two-year military assault that followed the 2023 Hamas attack on southern Israel.

items.title: Celine Cremer search volunteers to look for missing Tasmanian man Peter Willoughby
items.summary: A group of volunteers, which played a major role in finding the remains of a lost Belgian tourist earlier this year, will today begin searching for another missing person in Tasmania's wilderness.

items.title: Major wine retailer Endeavour Group retreats from production amid industry pressure
items.summary: Endeavour Group, which owns Dan Murphy's and BWS, announces it will step away from some key vineyards and wineries in South Australia, Victoria and Tasmania, and close a major South Australian bottling plant.

items.title: Officeworks to offshore hundreds of Sydney and Melbourne jobs to India and Philippines
items.summary: The Australian office products retailer is planning to offshore hundreds of jobs to India and the Philippines, joining a growing trend of businesses hoping to find more highly skilled white-collar workers overseas.

items.title: Letter alleges 'unlawful conspiracy' to oust new APY Lands general manager
items.summary: The new manager of a remote South Australian Aboriginal land corporation has alleged there is an "unlawful conspiracy" to oust him, but the board says his position has already been terminated.

items.title: Patient advocates hope review will improve travel subsidy rates and response times
items.summary: Kevin O'Neill says Queensland's patient travel subsidy scheme left him stranded with a dangerous medical condition 300 kilometres from home. Patient advocates say it is one of a litany of failures they hope will be fixed in a review of the scheme.

items.title: Australia focused on making winning start at Valladolid leg of World Championship rugby sevens
items.summary: The threat of relegation hangs over the Australian men's rugby sevens squad ahead of the final two rounds of the World Championship series.

items.title: Private landholders hand three blocks in Tasmania to Aboriginal Land Council
items.summary: It has been two decades since substantial land hand-backs in Tasmania, so private owners are doing it instead. Near Hobart, a piece of land that was once a dumping ground is being restored.

items.title: Aggreko building Australia's largest hybrid power plant in outback Queensland in light of CopperString delays
items.summary: An international energy company is building Australia's largest off-grid renewable hybrid power facility, as a state politician criticises the need for it.

items.title: Austrian man jailed for 15 years for Taylor Swift concert terror plot
items.summary: A man who plotted but ultimately failed to launch a terrorist attack at Taylor Swift concert in Vienna, forcing the pop star to cancel three performances, is sentenced to 15 years behind bars.
